title: chamaecrista tortuensis
intro: Chamaecrista tortuensis, also known as the twisted cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chamaecrista tortuensis include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating pain, as well as being used to manage diabetes and various gastrointestinal issues. Chamaecrista tortuensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
